Gorgon's head.
Romano Celtic Head from Pediment of the Temple of Sulis Minerva, Roman Baths, Bath.

"The David Kirby were going to Sarah's 50"
birthday party. We all walked to see the
photo_captions: Gorgon's head. Romano Celtic Head from Pediment of the Temple of Sulis Minerva, Roman Baths, Bath.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a large photo of the Gorgon's head / Romano-Celtic carved stone head. Caption is handwritten below the photo. Below caption, narrative text begins (continues onto right page): 'The David Kirby were going to Sarah's 50" birthday party. We all walked to see the'. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The right edge of the image shows partial text that is the beginning of lines on the right page — not double-transcribed.

pages.full_text:
Roman Baths, which we enjoyed. Melody & Charlotte hadn't heard much about the Romans.

   We had a filling Turkish supper at Marmaris & caught 2 taxis back to the campsite.

      __Monday 30th August__

   We all slept well & ate breakfast outside. Dylan & Mia slept in after the party. We had a leisurely breakfast. Then it rained. So the children continued their 4 in a row & chess & cards in Simon's van. The adults chatted & drank coffee in David's van. We had lunch & went blackberrying. We left at 3.15.

      __Saturday 16th October__

   At about 11.45. we set off for Trimpley & then the farm. Thomas had arrived an hour earlier from America (Ohio). Simon, Eve, Thomas, Melody & Charlotte & Tonie visited Trimpley. Jim is very ill. We had sandwiches for lunch &
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 46 in top-right corner. Text continues from left page (narrative about Roman Baths visit). 'Monday 30th August' and 'Saturday 16th October' are underlined headings. Year cannot be determined from page content alone — no year is written on the page. Volume B covers 2003-2019 but these dates cannot be pinned to a specific year from the evidence on the page. 'Marmaris' is a coastal resort in Turkey; the campsite reference suggests an overseas camping trip. 'Trimpley' is a village near Bewdley, Worcestershire, UK. The text cuts off at the bottom mid-sentence. 'Sarah's 50th birthday party' mentioned on left page may help date the entries if Sarah's identity is known.
